summary: binding.gyp uses GYP command-expansion to execute shell at install time
details: The package ships a binding.gyp whose targets/sources field contains GYP command-expansion syntax (<!(...)) at line 6. npm implicitly invokes node-gyp rebuild whenever a binding.gyp is present — even with no declared install/postinstall script — and GYP evaluates <!(...) as a shell command during the configure step. The package is a Backstage LDAP auth backend plugin (pure TypeScript/JavaScript by purpose) and ships no native C/C++ source files that would justify a binding.gyp, so the file's only operational effect is to execute the embedded shell command on every `npm install`. The traced content additionally tripped the model-provider content filter, consistent with operational malware shape rather than a legitimate native-addon build configuration.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
